private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Constellr is a Series A space & aerospace company founded by Max Gulde and Marius Bierdel, having raised €75M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable method here, given data availability. The five-company median yields $400M, with a distribution spanning $274M (floor, Sift) to $1.3B (ceiling, Aalyria). This range reflects stage and market positioning variance across the subsector. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples—the Space & Aerospace public median sits at 45.3x across five tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL)—is not applicable due to absence of Constellr revenue disclosure, standard for private companies at this stage.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This reflects the peer median but acknowledges typical Series A positioning variance relative to later-stage peers in the dataset. Key uncertainties include Constellr's revenue run-rate, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term funding trajectory. The peer set skews toward later-stage valuations; a down-round adjustment would be warranted if growth metrics materially underperform the cohort.
